name: Ucero and its castle — circular from El Burgo de Osma
name_original: Ucero y su castillo — circular desde El Burgo de Osma
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma are set within a landscape characterized by the Soria highlands and the Duero river bank, with the Ucero and Abión rivers flowing nearby. The region features diverse terrain, including rocky cliffs and pine forests, particularly within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse both paved and unpaved segments, offering varied experiences from river valleys to elevated viewpoints. The area also incorporates historical elements, such as medieval castles and Romanesque hermitage sites. Best touring cycling routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma The most popular touring cycling route is Garmoz and its castle from El Burgo de Osma — circular via the El Cid Cycle Tourist Route, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge while exploring historical sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Langa de Duero from El Burgo de Osma — circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route, a difficult 44.1 miles (70.9 km) path. This route follows a historical path through varied Soria landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Gravel route between Burgo de Osma and the fortress of Gormaz, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail leading through the Soria highlands, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma is defined by river valleys, pine forests, and medieval fortresses, offering routes for various fitness levels. The network includes options for easy rides to more demanding routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy rides to more demanding journeys with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, characterized by the Soria highlands and the Duero river bank, with the Ucero and Abión rivers flowing nearby. You'll find routes traversing rocky cliffs, pine forests, and river valleys. Many routes are primarily paved, but some incorporate unpaved segments for those seeking a more rugged experience.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners and families. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14939196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Water Trough in Sotos loop from El Burgo de Osma&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.3 km long with minimal elevation gain, making it a pleasant option for a relaxed outing.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while cycling in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The area is rich in history. You can cycle past the medieval enclosure of Burgo de Osma, visit the reconstructed &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or challenge yourself to reach the formidable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bartolomé&lt;/a&gt; is another fascinating historical stop.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region offers breathtaking views, especially from elevated points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park provides panoramic vistas of the Soria and Burgos pine forests and the peaks of the Iberian System, making it a rewarding stop on your ride.

Q: Can I find natural parks or unique natural features on the routes?
A: Yes, a prime natural highlight is the &lt;strong&gt;Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, renowned for its impressive caves, rocky cliffs, and as a nesting site for eagles and griffon vultures. Routes often pass through or near this spectacular environment, offering opportunities to experience its unique geology and wildlife.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Many routes in the area are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041743324/garmoz-and-its-castle-from-el-burgo-de-osma-circular-via-the-el-cid-cycle-tourist-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garmoz and its castle from El Burgo de Osma — circular via the El Cid Cycle Tourist Route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041711968/langa-de-duero-from-el-burgo-de-osma-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Langa de Duero from El Burgo de Osma — circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some more challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041711968/langa-de-duero-from-el-burgo-de-osma-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Langa de Duero from El Burgo de Osma — circular via the Camino del Cid Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 70.9 km path following a historical route. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1502715211/gravel-route-between-burgo-de-osma-and-the-fortress-of-gormaz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel route between Burgo de Osma and the fortress of Gormaz&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 43.5 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The region is generally well-suited for cycling across various seasons. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred. Winter cycling is possible, but it's advisable to check local weather conditions for potential cold or snow, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and pubs. Burgo de Osma-Ciudad de Osma itself has a charming Baroque Main Square with various establishments. Planning your route with komoot can help you identify suitable stops for refreshments.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about the routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the combination of rich history, diverse natural environments, and the variety of routes catering to different fitness levels, from river valleys to medieval fortresses.

Q: What are the parking options for cyclists in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: Burgo de Osma-Ciudad de Osma, as a destination for outdoor activities, generally offers parking facilities within the town. Specific parking areas for cyclists are usually available near popular starting points or within the town center. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient options.
tips: El Burgo de Osma, then called Uxama, suffered for centuries under alternating domination and reconquest between the Moors and the Spaniards. While there is no historical evidence that the Spanish knight and national hero El Cid played a role in Osma's story during this period, it cannot be ruled out either, as he was the lord of the castle and landlord of neighboring Gormaz.
The climb is not easy (5-8%) but the effort is worth it. The views are very good! The castle is a medieval fortress, owned by the Catholic church.
